Title: A cytochrome P450 CYP71 enzyme expressed in Sorghum bicolor root hair cells participates in the biosynthesis of the benzoquinone allelochemical sorgoleone

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal hydroxylation step of phenolic lipid/sorgoleone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes dihydroxylation of 5-pentadecatrienyl resorcinol-3-methyl ether at the C4 and C6 positions of the resorcinol ring to form dihydrosorgoleone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major allelochemical contributing to Sorghum allelopathy; potential reduction in reliance on synthetic herbicides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily/preferentially expressed in root hair cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in isolated root hairs relative to total roots, panicles, stems, leaves, and shoot apices
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 8- to 10-day-old seedlings; root hair cell context; heterologous assays with supplied substrate
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 2×35S promoter (constitutive) for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase (in yeast WAT11 background)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR tissue profiling; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae with GC-MS product detection; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana microsomes; RNAi-mediated repression in Sorghum bicolor followed by GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dihydrosorgoleone formation and supports sorgoleone accumulation; RNAi repression reduces sorgoleone content in multiple independent sorghum transformants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AM1 was identified from a root-hair library as a P450 preferentially expressed in sorghum root hairs where sorgoleone is biosynthesized. 2. Recombinant CYP71AM1 converted 5-pentadecatrienyl resorcinol-3-methyl ether to dihydrosorgoleone in yeast and in transiently transformed Nicotiana benthamiana microsomes. 3. RNAi-mediated repression of CYP71AM1 in sorghum significantly reduced sorgoleone accumulation in five of eight independent events, supporting an in planta role in the terminal hydroxylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering sorgoleone content, allelopathic weed suppression, and plant-based bioproduction of sorgoleone/related phenolic lipids; may be stacked with upstream pathway genes for stronger flux control
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi did not eliminate sorgoleone completely, suggesting incomplete silencing or possible redundant CYP activity; altered sorgoleone may affect allelopathic potential
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root hair cDNA/transcript library mining; RT-qPCR expression analysis; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dihydrosorgoleone is the in vivo hydroquinone precursor that auto-oxidizes after secretion to yield sorgoleone; first reported P450 able to perform dihydroxylation on a resorcinol ring moiety

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: No detectable conversion of 5-pentadecatrienyl resorcinol-3-methyl ether in the yeast assay used
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major allelochemical contributing to Sorghum allelopathy
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in root hairs among tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in root hairs than other tested tissues by RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast heterologous assay with supplied substrate
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase (in yeast WAT11 background)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR tissue profiling; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae with GC-MS assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable hydroxylation activity toward the proposed physiological substrate in yeast; not supported as a sorgoleone-pathway enzyme in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AF1 was identified as a root-hair-preferential P450 candidate from sorghum. 2. Functional expression in yeast produced a spectrally active P450 protein. 3. Unlike CYP71AM1, CYP71AF1 did not metabolize 5-pentadecatrienyl resorcinol-3-methyl ether in the assay, so subsequent analyses focused on CYP71AM1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low immediate value for sorgoleone engineering based on current evidence; may warrant testing with other substrates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function may require different substrate/conditions and remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root hair cDNA/transcript library mining; RT-qPCR expression analysis; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally tested core candidate in Results, but evidence was negative for the focal substrate/product relationship
